[Comment 6] JIRAUSER62328 (Thu, 29 Jan 2026 17:08:08 +0100):

Security audit of dependencies revealed several dependencies having vulnerabilities. In scope of mitigating them was done:

  • lodash-es 4.17.22 -> 4.17.23
  • @pact-foundation/* - removed completely (see "pact tests" block below)
  • @compodoc/compodoc 1.1.11 -> 1.2.1
  • @stoplight/spectral-cli 6.11.0 -> 6.15.0
  • chai-json - removed completely (no usages in project)
  • @angular-eslint/* - updated to 19.8.1 to match angular version
  • @typescript-eslint/* - updated to latest 8.54.0 to satisfy other dependencies
  • eslint-plugin-jsdoc - 41.1.2 -> 62.3.1
  • webpack-dev-server 4.11.1 -> 5.2.3
  • angular-cli > pacote Angular team not going to fix vulnerability in v19 as it will break compatibility with node 19, since we already using node 22 we can use newer version of pacote which doesn't have vulnerability. => overriden pacote version in package.json

pact tests

Were disabled in 2023 in scope of https://d3.ce.capgemini.com/jira/browse/BNUVZ-5702, and not used since then.
Agreed with Aymen Mastouri that outdated dependencies needed for Pact tests could be removed, and all specs referencing them - excluded from builds (files remains in repo for now).

eslint

Update of eslint brought new linter rules and current codebase is non-compliant to some of them. Those rules were disabled for now, separate task was created for reenabling them and refactor code accordingly

[Comment 9] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 27 Jan 2026 15:26:00 +0100):

SASS compiler

Update to angular 19, brought sass@1.85.0 which marks usages of "@import" in scss files as deprecated. As we were using it extensively build- and browser consoles are spammed with warnings.

Was able to migrate to new syntax in stylesheets of components, however not in global stylesheets, which are importing 3rd party stylesheets we have no control over (boostrap4, bnotk/ds-ng).

According to [documentation](https://sass-lang.com/documentation/breaking-changes/import/) there is no plans to remove support of old syntax in nearest future, so we can just *silent* those warnings for now (checked with DS team - this approach was taken). But currently used builder(build-angular:browser) is not supporting configuration for sass. 

Builders

path a) keep webpack with @angular-devkit/build-angular:browser builder

pros:

  • less configuration changes needed, potentially less issues
    cons:
  • to silence sass builder warnings - need to provide custom-webpack config, as build-angular:browser doen't support configuration of sass loader
  • missing percs of modern builders (build speed, bundle size)
  • potential deprecation pressure as angular defaults to new builder and promotes its usage

path b) switch to @angular-devkit/build-angular:application

pros:

  • modern toolset - quicker builds, better dev experience
  • supports configuring sass builder out of the box

cons:

    On leads sync agreed that I'll prepare document describing options with new/old builder, potential consequences, pros/cons and that will be brought to customer to make an informed decision whether we invest time to migration or not.

    [Comment 14] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 14:21:58 +0100):

    ag-grid has quite extensive list of breaking changes, accommodating them will take some effort:
    https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/upgrading-to-ag-grid-32/#breaking-changes

    [Comment 15] fimisior (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 11:27:55 +0100):

    To upgrade Angular to version 21.x we have to also upgrade environment dependencies: Node.js, TypeScript.
     
    Current state on UVZ:
    node.js v20.18.0
    "typescript": "~5.4.5",
     
    Should be on UVZ:
    node.js v22.12.0
    "typescript": "5.9.0", (5.8.0 with Angular 19.2)
     
    From Angular documentation  RxJS should be set to ^6.5.3 or ^7.4.0, so we are fine with that because we are now using rxjs 7.4.0 version.
     
    This part is basing on:
    https://angular.dev/reference/versions
     
    We should remember to upgrade Angular step by step and verify if app works after each step (manual test on browser, also XNP smoke tests, unit tests and e2e tests should pass)
     
    Step 0: update node.js to  ^22.12.0, typescript to 5.8.0  

     Check if scripts are working fine locally and on Jenkins
    Step 1: Angular 18 to 19
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=18.0-19.0&l=3
    Step 2: Angular 19 to 20
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=19.0-20.0&l=3 
    Step 3: Angular 20 to 21
    Here we need to upgrade typescript to >=5.9.0 <6.0.0
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=20.0-21.0&l=3 
     
    After each step ensure that unit-test are passing and XNP works.
     
    Another topic is that UVZ has DS dependency on master.
    Design System (Pattern Library) relies on: Bootstrap, NG Bootstrap, AG Grid, NG Select, NG Select Highligh.
     
    Dependencies fo

migration_sequence.title: Standalone components are now default
migration_steps: Update test imports to use component directly

migration_sequence.title: node-sass -> Dart Sass (sass)
migration_steps: Replace node-sass with sass: npm uninstall node-sass && npm install -D sass
Add @use 'sass:math' in SCSS files using division
Test: ng build --watch to verify all SCSS compiles

migration_sequence.title: Browser builder -> Application builder
migration_steps: Remove buildOptimizer and vendorChunk (not needed with esbuild)

migration_sequence.title: TypeScript 5.4 -> 5.6
migration_steps: Fix any new strict type errors from TS 5.5/5.6

migration_sequence.title: ngIf/ngFor/ngSwitch -> @if/@for/@switch control flow
migration_steps: Remove CommonModule imports where only used for ngIf/ngFor

migration_sequence.title: APP_INITIALIZER -> provideAppInitializer
migration_steps: Update imports: remove APP_INITIALIZER, add provideAppInitializer

migration_sequence.title: Karma test runner -> Vitest/Web Test Runner
AG Grid compatibility check
migration_steps: Check AG Grid compatibility matrix for target Angular version
Review AG Grid changelog for breaking changes

migration_sequence.title: ng-bootstrap compatibility check
migration_steps: Review component API changes in changelog

migration_sequence.title: ngx-translate compatibility check
migration_steps: Consider migration to @angular/localize if ngx-translate is unmaintained

migration_sequence.title: @ViewChild -> viewChild() signal query
migration_steps: Update access patterns to use signal calls

migration_sequence.title: HttpClientModule -> provideHttpClient()
migration_steps: Replace HttpClientModule with provideHttpClient() in providers
Add withInterceptorsFromDi() if using class-based interceptors
Remove HttpClientModule from module imports array

pre_migration_checks: Run npm audit and address existing vulnerabilities
Backup current repository and create a release tag
Verify CI pipeline passes on current version
Confirm Node.js version compatibility (upgrade to Node 22 if needed)
post_migration_checks: Execute full unit and integration test suites
Run security scan on updated dependencies
Validate that vertical action bar still functions

implementation_steps: Update Node.js to version 22 and verify npm install succeeds
Upgrade TypeScript to 5.6 and fix any compilation errors
Apply migration sequence steps in order, starting with standalone component migration
Upgrade third‑party libraries (ng-bootstrap, ag-grid, ng-select) to versions compatible with Angular 19
Replace deprecated APP_INITIALIZER usage with provideAppInitializer
Migrate test runner from Karma to Vitest and adjust CI configuration
Convert all @Input, @Output, @ViewChild usages to signal based APIs
Remove any remaining Angular 18 or PL 11.3.1 specific code and verify vertical action bar compatibility

unit_tests: Verify each migrated component renders correctly with the new standalone format
Ensure signal‑based inputs and outputs emit and receive values as before
integration_tests: Test end‑to‑end flows involving ag‑grid and ng‑bootstrap components
Validate API communication using the updated HttpClient provisioning

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (ActionRestService, AbstractSecurityConfiguration, JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl)
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for state‑changing operations (AbstractSecurityConfiguration, KeyManagerRestService)
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException for generic error cases
Add @ExceptionHandler for Exception in global exception handler

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The migration involves extensive breaking changes (standalone components, control‑flow migration) and many deprecated APIs, but most third‑party libraries have compatible versions, keeping effort manageable.
risks: Breaking changes from standalone migration may require extensive refactoring of module boundaries
Potential incompatibility of third‑party libraries (ag‑grid, ng‑bootstrap) with Angular 19
Regression of UI behavior due to control‑flow template changes
